hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning molecular generation framework incorporates a dual-objective reward function that simultaneously maximizes pharmacophoric similarity (via CATS descriptors) to known estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity to existing compounds, then it will generate novel drug-like molecules with superior docking scores and drug-likeness properties compared to a baseline generative approach using only QED and docking scores, because encoding pharmacophore patterns rather than explicit structural features allows the model to explore chemically diverse scaffold space while preserving the functional binding characteristics essential for estrogen receptor activity.
